The execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:
  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add <specific-paths>...'

So agent self-commit is the contract. But:

(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.

(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.

  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. AC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.

AC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.

AC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.

AC5. What is done
- Verified the bead's stated hard-block precondition still holds: V9 cannot run for
  exactly the same infrastructure reasons that closed V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23) as no_changes.

  1. .agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/ does not exist. Only ddx and helix-*

     by the plan's generator pipeline is still missing.

     execution definition registered, and nothing lists any tool-graphic artifact
     under produces_artifact.
  3. `ddx artifact regenerate tools-composite` fails with
     "artifact \"tools-composite\" not found in document graph" — the artifact is
     not registered. selectArtifactGenerator (cli/cmd/artifact.go:99) would also
     error with "no generator definition or generated_by provenance".
  4. The V8 sibling bead (ddx-1848bbaa "author 4 tool-graphic prompts") is closed,
     but docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/ contains only the six principle prompts —
     no tool-graphic prompt files exist. There is no prompt input for V9 to consume
     even if generation infrastructure were available.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill (nano-banana-pro-openrouter) under
   .agents/skills/. Symlinks under .crush/skills/ and .pi/skills/ point at a
   directory that doesn't exist here.
2. No registered execution definition (`ddx exec list --json` is empty), so
   `ddx artifact regenerate` has no generator to dispatch to.
3. No registered tool-graphic artifacts in the document graph. Without sidecars
   declaring id + media_type + depends_on, regenerate can't resolve them.
4. No tool-graphic prompt sources in docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/, despite
   V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) being closed. V9's contract assumes those prompts exist.
5. The plan explicitly forbids manual prompt-file → image fallbacks
   (plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md:255). V9 must run through the artifact
   pipeline or not run at all.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. Re-open or supersede V8 (ddx-1848bbaa) — the four tool-graphic prompt files
   it was supposed to author are not in the worktree, so V9's input contract
   is also unmet. Verify the closure was substantive, not a no_changes.
2. Predecessor bead: install / vendor nano-banana-pro-openrouter into
   .agents/skills/ so the broken symlinks resolve.
3. Predecessor bead: author `ddx exec define` for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter
   generator with produces_artifact entries for each tool graphic plus
   tools-composite.
4. Predecessor bead: register the four tool-graphic artifacts and tools-composite
   in the document graph (sidecars with id, media_type image/png, depends_on
   the V8 prompts and DESIGN.md tokens).
5. Once 1–4 hold, V9 collapses to: budget-tracked sequential
   `ddx artifact regenerate <each-tool-graphic>` then
   `ddx artifact regenerate tools-composite`, evaluating each render against
   the bead's acceptance criteria (placement, contrast, mobile crop, alt text,
   file size, metaphor parses without copy, brand-fit / no-kitsch),
   halting at $1.50/image or $10 total.

Recommendation
File the four predecessor beads above, block ddx-297e7b4b on them, and re-queue.
Do not escalate to a stronger model — the constraint is missing infrastructure
and missing inputs, not model capability. This mirrors the V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23)
disposition exactly; resolving the infrastructure unblocks both the principles
and tools lenses simultaneously.

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260501T164908-85c06bf9/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

  <bead id="ddx-9c5bca8f">
    <title>bead/axon: implement backend operations with generated GraphQL client</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.
2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260427T000819-3d343cfa/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255. Same fundamental blocker as
B22d-f (ddx-9f4b1a8c, no_changes 2026-04-27): lefthook `runtime-lint`
hook (lefthook.yml) filters analyzer diagnostics to the set of staged
files, and the in-scope test files contain pre-existing RunOptions /
CompareOptions composite literals whose underlying APIs are explicitly
named out-of-scope by this bead's parent decomposition (step h owns
service_run.go; runner.go and claude_stream.go aren't in this step's
in-scope at all).

What is done (verified locally, then reverted)
- compare_adapter.go is straightforward to drain of RunOptions /
  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions:
    * RunFunc redeclared as `func(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,
      runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error)`.
    * defaultResolvePromptForCompare(runtime AgentRunRuntime).
    * runCompareArmWith(run RunFunc, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime,
      armIdx, harnessName, baseDir, prompt, worktreePath).
    * RunCompareWith(run, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime, resolvePrompt,
      cleanupSandbox).
    * RunQuorumWith(run, rcfg, runtime QuorumRuntime).
    * RunBenchmarkWith(runCompare func(config.ResolvedConfig,
      CompareRuntime) (*ComparisonRecord, error), suite).
    * RunCompareViaService / RunCompareWithAgent / RunQuorumViaService /
      RunQuorumWithAgent / RunBenchmarkViaService / RunBenchmarkWithAgent
      rewritten to take (rcfg, runtime) instead of CompareOptions /
      QuorumOptions and to delegate through the new RunFunc.
    * RunCompareWithConfigViaService / RunQuorumWithConfigViaService
      collapsed into a single forwarding hop (they no longer need to
      assemble a CompareOptions/QuorumOptions intermediary).
    * RunQuorumWithConfig retained, now calling RunQuorumWith with the
      new shape.
- models.go BenchmarkArmsToCompare changes return shape from
  CompareOptions to CompareRuntime (and accepts an AgentRunRuntime
  baseline rather than a RunOptions). compare_adapter.go's
  RunBenchmarkWith uses that shape directly.
- models_test.go updates the BenchmarkArmsToCompare call to pass
  `AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: "test"}` and assert
  CompareRuntime.{Harnesses, ArmModels, ArmLabels}. This is the only
  RunOptions-related drift in models_test.go — the rest of that file
  doesn't reference RunOptions.
- After the in-package edits, `cd cli && go build ./...` passes and
  `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` is clean once the test
  files in the next bullet are also updated.
- agent_test.go (out of scope) needs one closure-shape update at
  line 584 because RunFunc's signature changed; with that one-line
  diff applied locally, TestQuorumRunsAllHarnesses passes.

What is blocking (lint policy vs. bead scope)

1. claude_stream_test.go (in-scope) contains RunOptions composite
   literals at lines 206, 212, 218, 224, 291, and 324. These are all
   inside test cases that call helpers declared in claude_stream.go —
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(opts RunOptions, cfg Config) at
   claude_stream.go:284 and runClaudeStreamingFn(r, ctx, harness,
   "claude", "", opts RunOptions, "hi", "", 10*time.Second) at
   claude_stream.go:304/477/507. claude_stream.go is NOT in this
   bead's in-scope; B22d's per-step decomposition reserves it for a
   separate step. With claude_stream.go's RunOptions surface
   unchanged, the test cannot construct anything other than
   RunOptions{...} to drive resolveClaudeProgressLogDir, and the
   runtime-lint hook flags every one of those literals when the
   file is staged.

2. prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (in-scope) has three RunOptions
   composite literals:
     * line 78  — r.resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile: fixture})
                  drives Runner.resolvePrompt in runner.go:976.
                  runner.go is out of scope.
     * line 85  — defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})
                  drives the in-scope helper. CAN be updated.
     * line 112 — RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
                  drives service_run.go:104. service_run.go's public
                  API is explicitly named out-of-scope ("step h").
   Even after migrating line 85 and updating line 98's RunBenchmarkWith
   closure to the new (rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime) shape, the file
   still carries lines 78 and 112 as pre-existing RunOptions
   composite literals. runtime-lint scopes diagnostics by file (not
   by hunk), so the staged file fails the gate on lines that this
   bead is not authorized to touch.

3. agent_test.go (out of scope, but transitively required) — once
   RunFunc's signature changes, agent_test.go:584's
   `func(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` no longer matches
   RunQuorumWithConfig's RunFunc parameter, so the package fails to
   build. The minimal fix is one line, but agent_test.go has 18
   other RunOptions composite literals (BuildArgs call sites at
   lines 118, 130, 148, 157, 165, 188, 196, 209, 214, 677, 684,
   718, 727, 856, 864, 971, plus 678, 684) that are pre-existing,
   unrelated to this bead, and depend on BuildArgs (runner.go) being
   migrated separately. Staging agent_test.go would surface every
   one of those as a lint violation.

The runtime-lint hook is mandatory (per project policy: --no-verify is
forbidden). Diagnostic filtering is whole-file, not hunk-aware, so
"only edit my own lines" doesn't help when the file already contained
violations that this bead's named scope can't reach.

What a follow-up attempt would need (any of the following)

A. Re-decompose B22d-g to also include claude_stream.go (so
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir / runClaudeStreamingFn migrate off
   RunOptions in the same commit, draining claude_stream_test.go),
   AND service_run.go's RunViaServiceWith + runner.go's
   resolvePrompt (so prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go drains), AND
   agent_test.go's BuildArgs call sites (so the RunFunc signature
   change compiles cleanly across the package). This is essentially
   the previously-attempted atomic B22d scope (worker-3f7a's
   recommendation, applied 2026-04-26 in commit 97561a6e); the
   subsequent re-decomposition into steps a-h reintroduced this
   exact tension.

B. Land step h FIRST (and in fact most of the rest of the
   per-file decomposition) so that claude_stream.go, runner.go,
   service_run.go, and agent_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
   RunOptions usages, then run step g — at that point the in-scope
   test files have no out-of-scope violations and lint passes on the
   staged set. The current bead-queue ordering puts step g before
   step h, so this requires a tracker reordering.

C. Change runtime-lint's filter from whole-file to hunk-scoped (so
   pre-existing violations on lines this bead doesn't touch don't
   block the commit). This is a lint-policy change in lefthook.yml;
   not in this bead's scope and not authorized by the bead
   description.

D. Authorize the worker (model-side) to extend in-scope to cover
   the transitively-required files (claude_stream.go,
   runner.go.resolvePrompt + BuildArgs, service_run.go.RunViaServiceWith,
   and agent_test.go), the same way B22d itself was authorized in
   2026-04-26 (commit 97561a6e). The diff for that expansion is
   already prepared and verified locally; only the bead-scope
   constraint stands in the way.

The implementation is mechanical and verified locally; the blocker is
project-level lint policy versus per-step bead scope, not
implementation difficulty. No partial commit is justified, since
landing only the in-scope-clean half (compare_adapter.go +
models.go + models_test.go) breaks the package build by leaving
agent_test.go with an incompatible RunFunc closure, and updating
that closure trips the lint on agent_test.go's pre-existing 18+
RunOptions literals.
"}}}

.ddx/executions/20260506T043749-7c9fea7a/prompt.md:8:PROBLEM
No performance baseline exists for the documents/artifacts UI at realistic corpus size (2000 artifacts). Without a baseline, the caching work (ddx-4a7eed8c) cannot demonstrate improvement, and CI has no reference point for regression detection.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/ does not contain a scale fixture for 2000 artifacts.
- No measurement of first-paint cold/warm latency, scroll smoothness, or search latency at this scale has been recorded.
- ddx-b9993722 (FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract, a dep) established the measurement methodology; this bead applies it to the 2k scale.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).
- Record baseline measurements in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md.
- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).

.ddx/beads.jsonl:371:{"acceptance":"1. Document cache keyed by graph revision exists; cold request builds once and subsequent requests with same revision use cache.\n2. Concurrent requests for the same graph revision do not double-build (single-flight or equivalent).\n3. documents resolver supports a search parameter; filtered results come from cache.\n4. Cache invalidated on file change (mtime-based).\n5. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_HitOnSameRevision verifies warm request does not rebuild.\n6. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_MissOnRevisionChange verifies cache miss triggers rebuild.\n7. TestDocumentsResolver_Search_FiltersFromCache verifies search uses cached data.\n8. Cold + warm latency benchmarked via B7-C1 fixture (results committed to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/perf-cache-results.md).\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:01:41.765899444Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-4a7eed8c","depends_on_id":"ddx-5681cc57","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T07:01:41Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.\n- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.\n- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.\n- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.\n- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.\n- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.\n- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Caching for resolvers other than documents.\n- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).","id":"ddx-4a7eed8c","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:7","area:server","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-97335425","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:17:46.692603922Z"}

  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T055551-c841968e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T055551-c841968e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T055551-c841968e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T055551-c841968e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T055551-c841968e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

    <title>perf: 2k fixture + baseline non-gating measurement</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
No performance baseline exists for the documents/artifacts UI at realistic corpus size (2000 artifacts). Without a baseline, the caching work (ddx-4a7eed8c) cannot demonstrate improvement, and CI has no reference point for regression detection.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/ does not contain a scale fixture for 2000 artifacts.
- No measurement of first-paint cold/warm latency, scroll smoothness, or search latency at this scale has been recorded.
- ddx-b9993722 (FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract, a dep) established the measurement methodology; this bead applies it to the 2k scale.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create a synthetic 2000-artifact fixture under cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/.
- Measure: first paint cold/warm, scroll smoothness (frame rate under scroll), search latency (50ms goal from parent perf epic).
- Record baseline measurements in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md.
- Do NOT add a CI gate (baseline only — gate added in a later bead after caching lands).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching implementation (ddx-4a7eed8c).
- CI gate (deferred).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Synthetic 2000-artifact fixture exists at cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/scale/ (JSON or equivalent format loadable by the dev server).
2. Baseline measurements captured at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-baseline.md including: first-paint cold, first-paint warm, scroll smoothness, search latency.
3. No CI gate added.
4. Fixture loads without crash or timeout in Playwright e2e (test('2k fixture loads without crash', ...)).
5. bun run test:e2e green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:7, area:tests, kind:test</labels>

10	# Technical Design: Axon Bead-Tracker Backend
11	
12	## Status
13	
14	Draft. Specifies the integration between the DDx bead tracker
15	(FEAT-004 / SD-004 / ADR-004) and an external Axon entity-graph-relational
16	store as a pluggable storage backend behind the
17	`bead_tracker.backend` switch introduced in TD-027.
18	
19	## Background
20	
21	Source plan: `/tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md`. That document captured the
22	field-by-field schema mapping, operation mapping, migration plan, the
23	GraphQL-vs-gRPC wire-transport pivot, and the open questions that fed
24	the operator review. The locked decisions from that review are baked
25	into this TD; the deferred-default decisions are adopted as v1 policy
26	below. This TD does not relitigate those decisions — it polishes them
27	into an implementable design.
28	
29	Cross-repo coordination is tracked in bead `axon-82b6f7b2`
30	(DDx-on-axon adoption checklist; lives in the Axon repo's bead store).
31	That bead enumerates every Axon-side capability DDx adoption requires,
32	its current status (already-shipped / draft feature / open spike), and
33	which DDx adoption phase (alpha / beta / GA) gates on it. Treat
34	`axon-82b6f7b2` as the canonical cross-repo tracker; this TD only
35	summarises the gating logic in the **Adoption gates** section.
36	
37	Axon is a separate Rust project (`/home/erik/Projects/axon`) exposing an
38	entity-graph-relational store over GraphQL (primary), gRPC, REST, and
39	MCP. Backing stores are pluggable per deployment (PostgreSQL, SQLite,
40	FoundationDB, fjall) per Axon SPIKE-001. Axon ships an opinionated
41	`bead.rs` module with its own `BeadStatus` state machine; **DDx does
42	not consume that module** — DDx beads have a different status set
43	(`open`/`in_progress`/`closed`/`blocked` plus claim semantics), an
44	append-only events array, and bd/br JSONL interchange requirements that
45	do not match Axon's built-in shape. DDx instead defines its own
46	collection (`ddx_beads`) and JSON Schema and uses Axon as a generic
47	typed-entity store.
48	
49	## Locked decisions
50	
51	These are operator-approved and not negotiable inside this TD:
52	
53	| # | Decision | Implication for design |
54	|---|---|---|
55	| D1 | **Deployment**: separate axon-server, operator-managed. | DDx does **not** spawn or supervise Axon. `ddx-server` has no responsibility for Axon's lifecycle. The operator runs Axon via systemd / docker-compose / etc. and configures the connection string in `.ddx/config.yaml`. `ddx doctor` gains an Axon-reachability diagnostic. |
56	| D2 | **Auth**: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. | Reuses ADR-006 (tsnet authentication). No new auth machinery in DDx. Axon already supports ts-net in `crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs` (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags), so the integration is configuration-only on both sides. |
57	| D3 | **Events storage**: separate `ddx_bead_events` collection with `event_of` links (Option B). | Two collections: `ddx_beads` (the bead row) and `ddx_bead_events` (one entity per event). Bead row stays light; events scale per-bead independently. |
58	| D4 | **Offline posture**: refuse all bead ops with a clear error when Axon is unreachable. | Axon is the source of truth. No local read-through cache, no WAL, no degraded-mode reads. `ddx bead *` and `ddx work` fail loudly with `axon unreachable at <addr>: <error>; check connectivity / config`. |
59	| D5 | **Wire transport**: GraphQL only. | All DDx↔Axon traffic is GraphQL — queries and mutations over HTTP, subscriptions over WebSocket. No gRPC, no REST, no MCP from the DDx side. The Go client is generated by `genqlient` from a pinned schema-introspection snapshot. Rationale: Axon's GraphQL surface already covers entity CRUD, links, traversal, transactions, schema, and subscriptions; DDx is already a GraphQL server internally (`cli/internal/server/graphql_*.go`), so DDx↔Axon is wire-symmetric; subscriptions unlock live UI updates for `ddx work` (Story 8) without polling; no protoc/codegen toolchain enters the DDx build. The per-call wire-overhead advantage of gRPC is immeasurable at DDx scale (low thousands of beads); if batch-transaction throughput later becomes painful for one specific path, gRPC can be added there without revisiting this decision. |
60	
61	## v1 policy defaults (deferred decisions)
62	
63	These can be revisited if implementation surfaces a problem; they are
64	not load-bearing for the architecture:
65	
66	| Question | v1 policy | Rationale |
67	|---|---|---|
68	| Schema versioning | **DDx-side lazy migrate on read** until Axon's FEAT-017 (schema evolution) ships; switch to Axon-native schema migration once it lands. When DDx reads an entity at an older `schema_version`, it transforms in-memory, returns to the caller, and writes back at the new version on the next mutation. A future `ddx bead schema upgrade` command exists for breaking changes. | Avoids large eager-migrate batches; standard pattern. Migration to Axon-native schema evolution is a contained refactor inside `cli/internal/bead/axon/`. |
69	| `ddx bead ready` query | **Day 1: two-phase GraphQL.** `query { ddxBeads(status: open) { id, dependencies { id, status } } }` — first page filters to open beads; client checks each candidate's deps in the same response. Day 2 (after Axon's `axon-05c1019d` pattern-query support ships): single GraphQL query against an `isReady` computed/`@cypher` field. Benchmark on the 1100-bead archive-scale fixture. | Simplest correct implementation with a clear performance escalation path that aligns with the cross-repo dependency. |
70	| Backup / DR | **Operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.** `ddx bead export` is the documented logical backup format. | Avoids DDx duplicating Axon's DR concerns. Aligns with operator-managed deployment (D1). |
71	
72	## Architectural decision
73	
74	DDx defines a `ddx_beads` collection (and a companion `ddx_bead_events`
75	collection per D3) in Axon, each with a DDx-controlled JSON Schema
76	registered via the GraphQL `putSchema` mutation. Axon serves as a
77	generic entity-graph-relational store; bead semantics (state machine,
78	claim flow, ready-queue computation) live in `cli/internal/bead`.
79	Axon's opinionated `bead.rs` is informational only.
80	
81	Rationale (from source plan, recorded for the record):
82	
83	- Preserves the DDx schema (FEAT-004 / SD-004) without forcing Axon's
84	  status model onto DDx.
85	- Maintains bd/br interchange compatibility — `ddx bead export | bd
86	  import` round-trip is codified in
87	  `cli/internal/bead/schema_compat_test.go`.
88	- Lets HELIX and other plugins extend the bead `Extra` map without
89	  coordinating with Axon's `bead.rs` schema.
90	- DDx ships per-collection schemas as artifacts under
91	  `cli/internal/bead/schema/` and pushes them via `putSchema` on first
92	  use.
93	
94	## Wire transport
95	
96	Per D5, **GraphQL only**. DDx generates a typed Go client from Axon's
97	GraphQL schema using [`genqlient`](https://github.com/Khan/genqlient):
98	
99	- A pinned schema-introspection snapshot lives at
100	  `cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql` (committed; refreshed via
101	  `make refresh-axon-schema`).
102	- Hand-authored `*.graphql` query/mutation/subscription documents live
103	  alongside the snapshot under `cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/`.
104	- `make gen-axon-client` runs `genqlient` and writes the generated
105	  Go bindings to `cli/internal/bead/axon/generated.go`.
106	- Schema drift between the pinned snapshot and the live Axon endpoint
107	  surfaces in CI (a `ddx doctor`-equivalent introspection compare run
108	  in CI fails the build on shape changes).
109	
110	Subscriptions ride a single long-lived WebSocket against the
111	`subscription_endpoint` (the standard `graphql-ws` / `graphql-transport-ws`
112	sub-protocol; pick whichever Axon's `crates/axon-graphql` server
113	implements). The first user of subscriptions is the `ddx work` live-update
114	feed (Story 8).
115	
116	Axon version pin: the build pins a specific Axon release tag (e.g.
117	`axon@v0.x.y`) and tracks Axon's CHANGELOG. Axon is pre-release; the
118	GraphQL schema is not yet frozen, so the pin plus the CI drift check
119	are mandatory.
120	
121	## Schema mapping
122	
123	DDx bead record (per `cli/internal/bead/marshal.go` and
124	`cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json`) → Axon entity in
125	collection `ddx_beads`:
126	
127	| DDx bead field | Axon mapping |
128	|---|---|
129	| `id` | Axon `EntityId` (string; preserves the existing `ddx-<8hex>` form) |
130	| `title` | `data.title` |
131	| `description` | `data.description` |
132	| `acceptance` | `data.acceptance` |
133	| `issue_type` (task/bug/epic/chore) | `data.issue_type` (string enum) |
134	| `status` (open/in_progress/closed/blocked) | `data.status` (string enum) — validated by the registered JSON Schema; **not** routed through Axon's lifecycle/`TransitionLifecycle` (DDx owns the state machine) |
135	| `priority`, `labels`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `assignee`, `claimed_at`, `claimed_pid` | `data.*` (verbatim field-for-field) |
136	| `parent` | Axon **link** of type `parent`, child → parent entity |
137	| `dependencies` | Axon **links** of type `depends_on`, this → each dependency target |
138	| `events` | per D3, **Option B**: each event is its own entity in `ddx_bead_events`, linked back to the bead via an `event_of` link. The bead row's `data` does **not** carry the events array. Export materialises the inline events array for bd/br interchange (see Operation mapping). |
139	| `Extra` (HELIX/plugin custom fields) | `data.extra` with `additionalProperties: true` at that path; preserves round-trip |
140	| `version` (optimistic concurrency) | GraphQL `Entity.version` (returned on every read; sent as `expectedVersion` on mutation) |
141	
142	### Schema artifacts
143	
144	Two new schema files, both under `cli/internal/bead/schema/`:
145	
146	- `bead-record-axon.schema.json` — extends `bead-record.schema.json`,
147	  registered as the schema for collection `ddx_beads`.
148	- `bead-event-axon.schema.json` — schema for collection
149	  `ddx_bead_events`. Fields: `event_id`, `at`, `kind`, `payload`, plus
150	  `event_of` link target.
151	
152	Both schemas carry an explicit `schema_version` (starts at `1`).
153	Schema upgrades push a new version via `putSchema`; live entities
154	migrate lazily on read per the v1 policy until Axon FEAT-017 lands.
155	
156	## Operation mapping
157	
158	All entries are GraphQL operations against the endpoint configured in
159	`.ddx/config.yaml`. Mutation field names below mirror Axon's existing
160	GraphQL surface (`crates/axon-graphql/`); the exact field spelling is
161	adopted from the pinned schema snapshot at code-gen time and may shift
162	per Axon's pre-release CHANGELOG.
163	
164	| DDx operation | GraphQL operation(s) |
165	|---|---|
166	| `ddx bead create` | `mutation { createEntity(collection: "ddx_beads", data: ..., links: [{type: "depends_on", to: ...}]) }` — link writes ride the same mutation via Axon's batched-mutation / `commitTransaction` field if multiple links |
167	| `ddx bead show <id>` | `query { ddxBead(id: ...) { id, version, data, parent { id }, dependencies { id, status }, events: linksIncoming(type: "event_of") { id, data } } }` — single round-trip via nested traversal |
168	| `ddx bead update` | `mutation { updateEntity(id: ..., expectedVersion: V, data: ...) }` (OCC) |
169	| `ddx bead close` | `updateEntity` (status → closed) — fused with the closing-event write inside one `commitTransaction` mutation |
170	| `ddx bead claim` | `updateEntity` (status → in_progress + `claimed_at` + `claimed_pid`) — atomic via OCC: on `expectedVersion` mismatch, the claim lost the race and the caller retries (or surfaces "claimed by another worker") |
171	| `ddx bead ready` | per v1 policy: two-phase GraphQL (status filter, then per-candidate dep status check fused into the same response shape via nested traversal). Day 2 escalation path is the `isReady` field once axon `axon-05c1019d` ships. |
172	| `ddx bead blocked` | symmetric to ready: query `status=open` then filter to those with at least one non-closed dependency; Day 2 uses a `blockedBy` computed field |
173	| `ddx bead dep add/remove` | `createLink` / `deleteLink` mutations (link type `depends_on`) |
174	| `ddx bead dep tree` | `query { traverse(from: ..., type: "depends_on", direction: outgoing, transitive: true) { ... } }` |
175	| `ddx bead evidence add` | `commitTransaction` mutation: `createEntity(collection: "ddx_bead_events")` + `createLink(type: "event_of")` |
176	| `ddx bead export` | iterate `query { entities(collection: "ddx_beads", cursor: ...) }`, fan-out to events via `linksIncoming(type: "event_of")`, materialise the inline events array, marshal to bd/br JSONL — must round-trip per FEAT-004 + `schema_compat_test.go` |
177	| `ddx bead import` | reverse: parse JSONL, split into bead row + event entities, `commitTransaction` per bead |
178	| `ddx work` live-update feed | `subscription { changeEvents(collection: "ddx_beads") { ... } }` over WebSocket — replaces polling for the worker UI (Story 8) |
179	
180	### Concurrency model
181	
182	Axon's `expectedVersion` (OCC) plus `commitTransaction` (ACID over
183	batched mutations) replace **both** of the current JSONL backend's
184	serialization mechanisms:
185	
186	- the `flock` on `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (acquired via
187	  `JSONLBackend.WithLock` per `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:74`)
188	- the git tracker-commit lock that motivated bead `ddx-da11a34a` (the
189	  v1 patch in `cli/internal/bead/lock.go` serializing
190	  `git add .ddx/beads.jsonl && git commit`)
191	
192	Multi-worker concurrency falls out for free — each DDx worker is just
193	another GraphQL client. The `git add .ddx/beads.jsonl && git commit`
194	step retires entirely under the Axon backend: bead state lives in
195	Axon, not on disk under git.
196	
197	### Multi-machine story (Story 14 prerequisite)
198	
199	FEAT-026 / Story 14 (per ADR-007 federation topology) needs a single
200	authoritative bead store that multiple machines can read and mutate
201	without git as the synchronisation channel. The JSONL backend cannot
202	satisfy that contract — it relies on a shared filesystem and a single
203	git remote as the merge point, and ADR-021 already calls out that the
204	Axon path "gets the right answer for free" for the multi-node story.
205	This TD is the prerequisite work: once `bead_tracker.backend: axon` is
206	the configured backend, every DDx worker on every machine speaks
207	GraphQL to the same Axon endpoint and OCC handles cross-machine races
208	identically to single-machine cross-process races. No new federation
209	machinery is needed inside DDx; FEAT-026 reduces to "operators run one
210	Axon and point all `.ddx/config.yaml` files at it" plus the auth story
211	in D2 (localhost / tsnet via ADR-006).
212	
213	## Deployment and configuration
214	
215	`.ddx/config.yaml` schema additions:
216	
217	```yaml
218	bead_tracker:
219	  backend: axon            # one of: jsonl (current default), bd, axon
220	  axon:
221	    endpoint: http://localhost:8080/graphql           # GraphQL HTTP endpoint
222	    subscription_endpoint: ws://localhost:8080/graphql  # WebSocket for subscriptions
223	    database: ddx-default        # Axon database name
224	    namespace: project-<name>    # one Axon namespace per DDx project
225	    collection: ddx_beads        # default; configurable
226	    events_collection: ddx_bead_events
227	    schema_version: 1
228	    auth:
229	      mode: localhost            # 'localhost' | 'tsnet'
230	      tsnet:                     # only when mode=tsnet, per ADR-006
231	        hostname: ddx-<project>
232	        # remaining tsnet fields per ADR-006
233	```
234	
235	`auth.mode: localhost` requires `endpoint` to resolve to a loopback
236	host; DDx refuses to dial non-loopback endpoints without `mode: tsnet`
237	(matches the security stance in `concerns.md`).
238	
239	### `ddx doctor` diagnostic
240	
241	`ddx doctor` adds a `bead-tracker` section. When `backend: axon` is
242	configured, the diagnostic:
243	
244	1. Resolves `endpoint` and issues a GraphQL `query { __typename }`
245	   ping with a 2s timeout.
246	2. Issues `query { describeCollection(name: "ddx_beads") { ... } }` and
247	   the same for `ddx_bead_events`.
248	3. Verifies that the registered schema version matches the configured
249	   `schema_version`.
250	4. Surfaces actionable errors: unreachable, collection missing, schema
251	   missing, schema version mismatch.
252	
253	Failure of the doctor check does **not** automatically initialize Axon
254	state — operators run `ddx bead migrate --to=axon` (see Migration plan)
255	which is responsible for collection/schema bring-up.
256	
257	## Migration plan
258	
259	Implements bead `ddx-155204fd` (B_MIGRATE). Command:
260	`ddx bead migrate --to=axon`.
261	
262	1. **Pre-flight**: ping Axon GraphQL; verify or `putSchema` for both
263	   collections; `createCollection` if absent.
264	2. **Read source**: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`,
265	   and `.ddx/attachments/*` (externalised events from
266	   `ddx bead migrate` under the JSONL backend, per ADR-004).
267	3. **Per bead**, in one `commitTransaction` mutation:
268	   - `createEntity(collection: "ddx_beads")` with the mapped data
269	   - `createLink(type: "depends_on")` for each dependency
270	   - `createLink(type: "parent")` if applicable
271	   - For each event: `createEntity(collection: "ddx_bead_events")` +
272	     `createLink(type: "event_of")`
273	4. **Verify**: pre-migration bead count = post-migration count; sample
274	   N=20 random beads and hash-compare `data` plus traversed link sets.
275	5. **On success**: rename `.ddx/beads.jsonl` →
276	   `.ddx/beads.jsonl.pre-axon-migration.bak` (retained for the rollback
277	   window; not deleted).
278	6. **Switch** `bead_tracker.backend: axon` in `.ddx/config.yaml`.
279	
280	The migration is idempotent: re-running on a partially or fully
281	migrated store is a no-op via OCC version checks plus presence checks
282	on the target entity ids.
283	
284	## Test plan
285	
286	1. **Backend conformance**: parameterise the existing
287	   `cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go` suite (10+ chaos tests) by backend
288	   selector (`jsonl`, `axon`); the Axon-backed runs must pass identically.
289	2. **Unit/integration** under `cli/internal/bead/axon/`:
290	   - `integration_test.go`: round-trip create → get → update → claim →
291	     close → re-read against a real Axon test instance
292	   - concurrent-claim race: two goroutines race a claim on the same
293	     bead; exactly one wins; the loser observes a version mismatch
294	   - dependency add/remove with traversal verification
295	   - export → bd-shape JSONL → import round-trip; assert byte-equal
296	     after a re-export (per `schema_compat_test.go`)
297	   - subscription smoke: open a `changeEvents` subscription, perform
298	     CRUD, assert the expected events arrive in order
299	3. **End-to-end**: spawn 2 DDx workers against a shared Axon, queue 50
300	   trivial beads, both drain to closure with no contention errors —
301	   replaces the workaround in `ddx-da11a34a`.
302	4. **Performance benchmark**: queue ops over the existing 1100-bead
303	   archive-scale fixture; assert parity-or-better against the JSONL
304	   backend on `bead ready`, `bead show`, and `bead create`. Failure
305	   here triggers the v1-policy escalation path for `bead ready` (the
306	   `isReady` field once axon `axon-05c1019d` lands).
307	5. **Doctor diagnostic**: `ddx doctor` surfaces each of the four
308	   failure modes (unreachable, missing collection, missing schema,
309	   schema version mismatch) with the expected message.
310	6. **Offline posture (D4)**: with Axon stopped, every `ddx bead *`
311	   subcommand and `ddx work` exit non-zero with the
312	   `axon unreachable at <addr>` message; no local writes occur.
313	7. **Schema drift CI**: introspect the live Axon endpoint pinned by
314	   the build, diff against the committed
315	   `cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql`, fail the build on shape
316	   changes (forces a deliberate `make refresh-axon-schema` + version
317	   bump).
318	
319	## Backend interface (Go)
320	
321	The current `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` interface is too coarse for
322	Axon — `WriteAll(beads []Bead)` cannot reasonably be implemented
323	against a remote entity store, and `WithLock` assumes a process-local
324	mutex around an in-memory rewrite. This TD replaces the existing
325	interface with a per-entity surface that both backends can satisfy.
326	Existing JSONL semantics are preserved by a thin adapter that wraps
327	the existing `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`/`flock` pair.
328	
329	```go
330	// cli/internal/bead/backend.go
331	package bead
332	
333	import "context"
334	
335	// Backend is the storage contract for the bead tracker. Both the
336	// JSONL backend (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go) and the axon
337	// backend (cli/internal/bead/axon/) implement this surface. The
338	// chaos tests under cli/internal/bead/ run against any registered
339	// Backend via newTestStore(t, BackendName).
340	type Backend interface {
341	    // Init prepares storage (create JSONL file + lock dir; or for
342	    // axon: ensure collections exist and registered schema is current).
343	    Init(ctx context.Context) error
344	
345	    // Get returns one bead with its OCC version. ErrNotFound for missing.
346	    Get(ctx context.Context, id string) (Bead, Version, error)
347	
348	    // List returns all beads in the tracker collection (open + in_progress
349	    // + closed not yet archived). Order is unspecified.
350	    List(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
351	
352	    // Create persists a new bead. Bead.ID may be empty; backend assigns
353	    // a ddx-<8hex> id and returns the assigned id. Fails if id is set
354	    // and already exists.
355	    Create(ctx context.Context, b Bead) (string, Version, error)
356	
357	    // Update applies mutate to the bead identified by id under
358	    // optimistic concurrency control: read at version V, apply mutate,
359	    // write iff stored version == V. ErrVersionMismatch on conflict;
360	    // caller retries. Used by claim, status transitions, and any
361	    // field edit. Backend MUST serialize concurrent Update calls on
362	    // the same id (jsonl: via flock; axon: via expectedVersion).
363	    Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead) error) error
364	
365	    // AppendEvent records an immutable audit event linked to a bead.
366	    // For jsonl: appends to the inline events array under the same
367	    // lock as the bead row. For axon: createEntity in
368	    // ddx_bead_events + createLink(event_of) in one commitTransaction.
369	    AppendEvent(ctx context.Context, beadID string, ev Event) error
370	
371	    // AddDep / RemoveDep maintain the depends_on graph. For jsonl:
372	    // mutate Dependencies on the bead row. For axon: createLink /
373	    // deleteLink with type=depends_on.
374	    AddDep(ctx context.Context, fromID, toID string) error
375	    RemoveDep(ctx context.Context, fromID, toID string) error
376	
377	    // Ready returns beads with Status==open whose every dependency
378	    // is Status==closed. Implementations are free to compute this
379	    // server-side; the JSONL implementation does it client-side; the
380	    // axon implementation uses the v1 two-phase GraphQL path until
381	    // axon-05c1019d ships an isReady computed field.
382	    Ready(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
383	
384	    // Close is sugar for Update(id, b -> b.Status = closed) plus
385	    // AppendEvent(closed). Provided so the backend can fuse both
386	    // writes into a single transaction (axon) or single locked
387	    // rewrite (jsonl).
388	    Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error
389	
390	    // Export streams beads in bd/br JSONL interchange shape (events
391	    // materialised inline). Round-trip with Import is verified by
392	    // schema_compat_test.go.
393	    Export(ctx context.Context, w BeadWriter) error
394	    Import(ctx context.Context, r BeadReader) error
395	}
396	
397	// Version is an opaque OCC token (jsonl: monotonic int from the
398	// in-memory snapshot; axon: GraphQL Entity.version).
399	type Version uint64
400	
401	var (
402	    ErrNotFound        = errors.New("bead: not found")
403	    ErrVersionMismatch = errors.New("bead: version mismatch (concurrent update)")
404	    ErrBackendOffline  = errors.New("bead: backend unreachable")
405	)
406	```
407	
408	The `BackendType` constants in `backend.go` extend with
409	`BackendAxon = "axon"`. The factory in `cli/internal/bead/registry.go`
410	selects the implementation from `bead_tracker.backend` in
411	`.ddx/config.yaml`. The existing `JSONLBackend` becomes an internal
412	adapter behind this interface; call sites in `cli/internal/bead/` and
413	`cli/cmd/bead_*.go` migrate to the new methods (mechanical change —
414	the operations already exist, just on `Store` rather than `Backend`).
415	
416	## Chaos contract the new backend must satisfy
417	
418	The contract under `cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go` is the
419	parameterised conformance suite for any `Backend` implementation. The
420	tests already operate on a `Store` value via `newTestStore(t)`; this TD
421	parameterises that helper by backend selector
422	(`newTestStore(t, BackendJSONL)` / `newTestStore(t, BackendAxon)`) and
423	both runs MUST pass identically. The following invariants are the
424	contract surface:
425	
426	| Test | Invariant the backend must guarantee |
427	|---|---|
428	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety` | 100 concurrent `Create` calls from 10 goroutines all succeed and all 100 beads are present afterwards with unique ids. No duplicate ids, no lost writes, no torn rows. |
429	| `TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions` | 5 goroutines × 20 cycles of `Update(status)` on the same bead leave the bead with a valid status (`open` / `in_progress` / `closed`) and the store fully readable. Transient `ErrVersionMismatch` is acceptable (caller's retry concern); corruption is not. |
430	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend` | A `Close(idA)` racing a `Create(B)` never loses either op: B exists with the right title; A's row remains parseable. Reproduces the original JSONL bug; required of every backend. |
431	| `TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost` | After a `Close(idA)` racing a `Create(B)` completes, `Get(idA).Status == closed` deterministically. The backend MUST serialize the read-modify-write of A (jsonl: flock; axon: OCC + retry on mismatch). The "close is never lost" invariant is the strict regression test for the JSONL bug and the acceptance criterion for axon. |
432	| `TestChaos_JSONLRoundTripIntegrity` | Renamed to `TestChaos_RoundTripIntegrity` once parameterised. Beads with unicode, emoji, embedded JSON-injection bytes, tabs, multi-line descriptions, and 200/1000/500-char field maxima survive `Create → ReadAll` field-for-field (Title, Description, Notes, Priority, Status, Labels). Validates that the wire format and schema preserve all `Bead` fields without truncation or escape-sequence loss. |
433	| `TestChaos_*` (all of the above) | No partial writes are visible to a concurrent reader. For jsonl this is the `writeAtomicFile` + `flock` guarantee; for axon this is the `commitTransaction` ACID guarantee. |
434	
435	Companion suites that the axon backend must also pass once
436	parameterised: `chaos2_test.go`, `property_test.go`, `fuzz_test.go`,
437	`merge_test.go`, and `schema_compat_test.go` (the bd/br JSONL
438	round-trip). `schema_compat_test.go` is the strictest
439	interoperability check — `Export` then `Import` then `Export` must
440	produce byte-equal output regardless of backend.
441	
442	## Archive policy under axon
443	
444	ADR-004 specifies that closed-bead events are externalised to
445	`.ddx/attachments/` and that eligible closed beads move to
446	`.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` to keep the active tracker file small. Under
447	the axon backend the externalisation problem disappears: events are
448	already separate entities in `ddx_bead_events` (per D3), so the active
449	`ddx_beads` row never carries the events array on the wire. The
450	"archive" distinction collapses to a soft index: `ddx bead migrate`
451	under the axon backend is a no-op for archive-split purposes; closed
452	beads remain in the same `ddx_beads` collection, distinguished only by
453	`status=closed`. Queries that historically scanned only the active
454	file (`bead list`, `bead ready`) become GraphQL `entities(collection:
455	"ddx_beads", filter: { status: ... })` calls and pay nothing for
456	closed-bead volume **once Axon FEAT-013 (secondary indexes) lands**.
457	Until then, status-field filtering is a table scan and is acceptable
458	for archives up to a few thousand beads (alpha gate). The attachments
459	mechanism remains the canonical location for non-event artifacts
460	(large logs, screenshots) referenced from event payloads; nothing in
461	this TD changes the attachments contract.
462	
463	| Current pain (JSONL backend) | Axon backend fix |
464	|---|---|
465	| Multi-worker `.git/index.lock` contention (bead `ddx-da11a34a`) | No JSONL writes, no git tracker commits — gone. |
466	| `beads.jsonl > 5 MB` lefthook complications | No JSONL on disk; growth is an Axon-side concern. |
467	| ADR-004 archive split + attachment store complexity | Subsumed by the two-collection model + per-event entities. |
468	| Multi-machine sync (today is git-based per FEAT-023) | Axon server is the source of truth; workers are clients. Federation falls out. |
469	| Implicit schema enforcement | Axon validates per-entity against the registered JSON Schema. |
470	| Audit trail is event-array-on-bead | Events are first-class entities; Axon's per-entity immutable audit augments. |
471	| OCC is flock-only | OCC via `expectedVersion` end-to-end. |
472	
473	## Cross-repo coordination
474	
475	Tracking bead: **`axon-82b6f7b2`** (DDx-on-axon adoption checklist;
476	lives in the Axon repo's bead store). It owns the full list of
477	Axon-side capabilities required for DDx adoption, current status, and
478	gating relationships to DDx adoption phases. This TD names only the
479	items relevant to the DDx-side design choices below; consult
480	`axon-82b6f7b2` for live status.
481	
482	| Axon-side item | Status (at time of this TD) | DDx-side impact |
483	|---|---|---|
484	| ts-net auth (D2) | **Already shipped** — Axon `crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs` supports Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags. | No coordination needed. DDx config simply selects `auth.mode: tsnet`. |
485	| GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions (D5) | **Already shipped** in `crates/axon-graphql/`. | Day 1 wire transport works against current Axon. |
486	| Multi-database / multi-namespace | **Already shipped.** | DDx maps one Axon namespace per DDx project (config above). |
487	| `axon-05c1019d` — GraphQL pattern-query support (P2 in axon) | Open. | Needed for single-query `ddx bead ready` / `ddx bead blocked`. **Day 1 ships with the two-phase GraphQL fallback**, so this is not blocking for alpha. Required for the `isReady`/`blockedBy` Day-2 escalation. |
488	| `FEAT-013` — secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon) | Draft. | Status-field query performance depends on this at >1000-bead scale. Acceptable to ship alpha with table-scan filtering; required before beta. |
489	| `FEAT-017` — schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon) | Draft. | Native schema migration. **Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migrate on read** (v1 policy above), so this is not blocking for alpha or beta. Required before GA so DDx schema upgrades stop accumulating ad-hoc migration code. |

491	## Adoption gates
492	
493	Mapping DDx adoption phases to the cross-repo dependencies above
494	(authoritative gating list lives in `axon-82b6f7b2`; this table is the
495	TD-side view).
496	
497	| Phase | Required Axon capabilities | DDx-side acceptance |
498	|---|---|---|
499	| **Alpha** | Already-shipped surface (GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions, ts-net auth, multi-db/namespace). `axon-05c1019d` is **not** required (two-phase fallback ships with Day 1). | All chaos / property / merge / schema-compat tests pass under `BackendAxon`. End-to-end 2-worker drain of 50 beads at zero contention errors. Acceptable to run with table-scan `entities(filter: {status: ...})` performance. |
500	| **Beta** | Alpha set **plus** `FEAT-013` (secondary indexes) so status-field queries don't scan. | Performance benchmark on the 1100-bead archive-scale fixture meets parity-or-better vs. the JSONL backend on `bead ready`, `bead show`, `bead create`. |
501	| **GA** | Beta set **plus** `axon-05c1019d` (single-query ready/blocked via `@cypher`/`isReady`) **plus** `FEAT-017` (Axon-native schema evolution; DDx lazy-migrate-on-read code retires). | Day-2 `ddx bead ready` runs as a single GraphQL query. Schema upgrades land via Axon-native migration, not DDx-side lazy transforms. |
502	
503	Each gate's exit criteria additionally require that the cross-repo
504	tracker `axon-82b6f7b2` shows the corresponding axon-side beads
505	closed; DDx does not promote a phase based on the TD alone.
506	
507	## Out of scope
508	
509	- The git tracker-commit lock issue (`ddx-da11a34a`) becomes moot under
510	  the Axon backend, but the lock-fix bead remains relevant for the
511	  JSONL backend's lifetime as the fallback path.
512	- Axon's pre-release status: GraphQL schema, data model, and SDK
513	  surface are not frozen. DDx pins a specific Axon version, snapshots
514	  the introspection result at `cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql`,
515	  and tracks the CHANGELOG.
516	- Axon's Go SDK does not exist; DDx generates its own via `genqlient`
517	  from the pinned introspection snapshot.
518	- gRPC, REST, and MCP transports between DDx and Axon are explicitly
519	  out of scope per D5.

  <bead id="ddx-4a751095">
    <title>execute-loop: tighten already_satisfied gate to require AC structural test name match</title>
    <description>
Observed across multiple beads in agent v0.9.9 / v0.9.10 work (2026-04-25): worker classifies bead as already_satisfied when the regression test suite passes, even when the bead's acceptance criteria explicitly name structural test functions or properties that the implementation has not exercised.

## Concrete instances

- agent-9d120ece (route resolution): AC #1 was structural ('cmd/agent no longer contains provider-building helpers or route-provider wrapper types that depend on internal/core'). Regression tests passed because the AC also named a regression command. Bead closed already_satisfied — but cmd/agent/routing_provider.go and cmd/agent/provider_build.go still contained the forbidden helpers. False close.
- agent-1023f072 (boundary tightening): same pattern earlier — AC structural property unmet, regression command passing, close.
- agent-d9c358ba (smart-routing wiring): worker analysis correctly identified the bead was too broad and split it. Status still classified already_satisfied which is misleading — 'split deferred' is a different outcome from 'AC was already met.'

## Principle

already_satisfied should mean: 'the AC's specific test functions or named structural properties were exercised and produced the asserted outcome without any code change in this attempt.' It should NOT mean: 'some unrelated regression command passed.'

If the AC names test functions (e.g. 'go test . -run TestX'), those tests must have run during the worker's verification step. If the AC names a structural property (e.g. 'no field X', 'file Y deleted', 'ProviderEntry.SupportsTools is OR-permissive'), the property must be checked.

## Suggested fix

Parse the bead's acceptance criteria during worker verification. Extract:
- Named test function references ('TestFoo', 'go test . -run TestFoo')
- File-existence claims ('file X deleted', 'no longer contains')
- AST-level claims ('field X removed', 'no import of Y')

For already_satisfied to be valid:
- All named test functions must exist and pass.
- All file-existence claims must hold via filesystem check.
- AST-level claims must hold via parser pass.

If any check fails, classify as no_changes (or a new outcome class like ac_unmet_no_changes) so the loop unclaims and tries again rather than closing the bead.

## Out of scope

- Changes to the success / land_conflict / execution_failed / review_request_changes outcome classes.
- Adding new structural-claim parsers beyond what the AC explicitly names.
- Automatic AC-claim extraction beyond test names + file paths + simple AST patterns. Free-form prose stays operator-judgment territory.

## Cross-reference

Discipline rule documented in agent repo AGENTS.md 'Review and Verification Discipline' section. Local mitigation (verify before accept, reopen with notes) caught three false closes in this session.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. New test cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestAlreadySatisfiedRequiresAcStructuralCheck covers: bead with AC naming TestX; worker run does not exercise TestX; classification is NOT already_satisfied (instead no_changes or new ac_unmet class).
2. Test covers: bead with AC naming a deleted file path; file still exists; classification is NOT already_satisfied.
3. Test covers: bead with AC asserting a struct does not have field Y; field still exists; classification is NOT already_satisfied.
4. Test covers: positive case — bead with AC naming TestX; worker run exercises TestX and TestX passes; AC structural property holds; classification IS already_satisfied (no regression).
5. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 passes.
    </acceptance>

  <bead id=".execute-bead-wt-ddx-8d747049-20260505T023636-7669921a-b6bc9398">
    <title>bead: scaffold cli/internal/bead/axon genqlient client and subscriptions</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/axon/ exists with schema.graphql, query documents, genqlient config, and generated Go bindings.
2. TestAxonClient_SchemaBindingsCompile and TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream are added in cli/internal/bead/axon/.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/axon/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>

  <bead id="ddx-978efe2c">
    <title>test: two concurrent workers with a stale-but-alive lease must not both execute one bead (parallel-execution regression)</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM: No test covers the STAGGERED contention case that actually occurred (ddx-d740539d): worker A claims and is alive but its lease aged past TTL (mid-intake / mid-long-op), and worker B then scans and reclaims -> both execute. The existing TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead only covers a same-instant race with a FRESH lease. Real-world signature: an attempt.terminated reason=bead_closed_externally event in .ddx/attachments/ddx-d740539d/events.jsonl after two attempts overlapped ~16.6 min.

ROOT CAUSE: missing end-to-end exclusivity regression for the stale-but-alive-lease scenario. This bead pins the contract that C1 (ddx-d333ad8a, intake heartbeat) + C2 (ddx-ba93de9e, PID-death takeover) must satisfy.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a chaos/integration regression simulating the exact scenario; it must FAIL before C1+C2 and PASS after.

NON-SCOPE: the fixes themselves (C1/C2).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. New TestChaos_StaleButAliveLeaseDoesNotPermitSecondExecution (cli/internal/agent/, new _chaos/_liveness test): worker A claims a single ready bead and enters a long phase exceeding an overridden small HeartbeatTTL while A's process is alive; concurrently run worker B's pick+claim loop against the same store. Assert EXACTLY ONE worker executes (one execute-bead attempt / one attempt_id), B never claims, and NO attempt.terminated/bead_closed_externally event is produced for the bead.
2. The test fails (two attempts) if either C1 or C2 is reverted (note this in a comment).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestChaos_StaleButAliveLeaseDoesNotPermitSecondExecution' -count=1 green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>

2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.
- If a verification command is auto-backgrounded by the harness, wait for its completion before rerunning the same command in the same worktree.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
