Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had work-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. 3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"2bbb93e87a3920ca453101fdeaea9565f382c185","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.475161508Z","description":"PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-63780ef6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-63780ef6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-07-02T22:52:48Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"queue-rank":1790,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7e9e7432","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:34.845842756Z"}

Test 7 (RebaseConflict) depends on the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 being in place — it may be a t.Skip if the coordinator hasn't landed yet, with a TODO comment and a bead reference.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:50:02.413040703Z","description":"Build the integration test suite for work orchestration using the script harness + real git temp repos. This is the test gap the opus test-plan review identified as the most critical coverage hole: every "concurrency" or "merge race" test today uses in-memory fakes with per-worker orchestrators, so the shared-writer invariant the meta-bead design depends on is never actually exercised.

## Test infrastructure helper

Build a shared helper `newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T) (projectRoot, initialRev string)` in a new file `cli/internal/agent/integration_helper_test.go` that:

1. Creates a temp dir via t.TempDir()
2. Runs `git init` with scrubbed GIT_* env (so the test's own pre-commit hook env doesn't leak)
3. Creates and commits an initial seed commit on `main`
4. Seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with N ready beads (parameterized by test)
5. Returns projectRoot + the initial SHA

The helper must NOT inherit any GIT_DIR from the parent test environment.

## Test cases

Create cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go with these cases. Each runs against the script harness and real git:

1. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_SingleBead_AppendLine_Merged** — one bead, directive appends a line and commits, asserts git log main shows +1 commit, worktree is cleaned up, bead closed with closing_commit_sha.

2. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoOp_ClassifiedAsNoChanges** — directive no-op, asserts outcome=no-changes, bead stays open (below adjudication threshold), no commit on main.

3. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_DirtyWorktreeSynthesized** — directive creates files but omits explicit commit, asserts SynthesizeCommit fires, outcome=merged, single synthetic commit on main with the expected files.

4. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FailedExit_WithCommits_Preserved** — directive commits then set-exit 1, asserts outcome=preserved, refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<sha> exists, main unchanged.

5. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_LinearHistory** — seed 5 distinct beads, spawn 5 workers concurrently (single store, single projectRoot), assert `git log --merges main | wc -l` is 0, `git rev-list main ^initial | wc -l` is 5, every bead closed. **This is the ddx-6aa50e57 acceptance test that cannot be written today.**

6. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_TwoWorkersSameBead_ClaimedOnce** — seed 1 bead, spawn 2 workers against the same store, assert exactly one executes and the other reports no_ready_work. **This is the ddx-3315dce2 acceptance test.**

7. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_RebaseConflict_Preserved** — two beads both modify the same line in the same file, workers start from the same base, first lands cleanly, second gets rebase conflict and is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/. Main has exactly +1 commit, ref exists for bead 2. (Only passes once the land coordinator from ddx-8746d8a6 is in place.)

8. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_ContextCancelBetweenIterations** — two beads, cancel context after first completes, assert second bead is not claimed. **This is the regression for commit 21d16e6.**

9. **TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_NoChangesRationale_ClosesBeadFast** — directive writes a no_changes_rationale.txt citing a commit SHA, asserts the bead closes as already_satisfied without the 3-strike cooldown wait.

Each test runs in isolation, cleans up its temp dir, and completes in under 10s. The full suite runs in under 60s. Use `-race` to catch any coordinator goroutine races.

## Out of scope

- Server-layer wiring (that's bead C)
- Real-AI acceptance tests (gated tier 4)
- Multi-project isolation (a separate bead, not filed here)
- Cross-machine scenarios (multi-machine topology bead)","id":"ddx-6f6bf12a","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:integration","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"Integration tests for work orchestration using script harness + real git","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.671814475Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1083:{"acceptance":"1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":600,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:29.795710244Z"}

There is no automated post-merge review step validating the commit against the bead's AC. Real-world practice requires a second-opinion agent (typically a higher-capability tier) to produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.

## Principle

Review is on-by-default with zero flags. The tool picks a tier >= the implementation tier (defaulting to smart). The user overrides only in exceptional cases.

## Default flow

- Implementation agent runs at current tier (see ddx-dc231144 tier-based escalation)
- On successful merge, tool automatically renders a review prompt via 'ddx bead review' (ddx-fdf031c9)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier + 1, smart)
- Review result is structured JSON: { verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK, findings: [...] }
- APPROVE → close bead, attach review evidence
- REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen bead via 'ddx bead reopen' (ddx-44084b3e), append review body, let the tier-escalation policy pick the next attempt
- BLOCK → stop escalating, flag for human

## Rare overrides

- --no-review (skip review entirely — e.g., doc-only beads in a tight loop)
- --review-harness / --review-model (explicit reviewer override for debug)
- Per-bead opt-out via a 'review:skip' label on the bead

## Dependencies

- ddx-fdf031c9 (ddx bead review command) provides the review prompt template
- ddx-44084b3e (ddx bead reopen) provides the atomic reopen primitive
- ddx-518b2690 (plain --output text) makes extracting the review verdict trivial
- ddx-5bac3c6b (provider health probe) feeds the reviewer tier selection

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 work:
- qwen3-coder-next merged A1 with real AC gaps (missing round-trip tests, zero Postgres coverage, scope creep)
- Without review, the bead would have closed silently and the gaps would surface only at epic rollup — much later and much more expensive to fix
- Review took 41s + a few minutes of reading. Cheap relative to the real cost of shipping broken code downstream.","events_attachment":"ddx-a317208c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-a317208c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Critical gap discovered during axon ADR-018 work:

When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the REVIEW FINDINGS must be surfaced to the next execute-bead attempt. Today this fails silently because:

1. Review findings posted via 'ddx bead update --notes' are NOT included in the execute-bead prompt.md. Execute-bead threads only <description> and <acceptance> into the XML payload.

2. Therefore a reopen-via-notes does NOT communicate the review findings to the next agent. The agent reads the original description + AC, sees the existing code already matches the description, and declares 'already_satisfied' — auto-closing the bead.

3. The workaround I had to use was updating the DESCRIPTION directly with the review findings. But then the description is no longer a stable spec — it's a moving target carrying review history.

## Required behavior

When a bead is reopened with a review verdict attached, execute-bead's generated prompt MUST include the most recent REQUEST_CHANGES review findings as a distinct section, e.g.:

  <review-findings iteration="2" reviewer="claude-opus-4-6">
  BLOCKING: UserIdentity field 'subject' should be 'external_id' per ADR-018 §3
  PARTIAL: aud-array test asserts serde_json::Error instead of AuthError::CredentialMalformed
  ...
  </review-findings>

And the execute-bead instructions should read: 'If <review-findings> is present, address each BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Do not declare already_satisfied if any BLOCKING finding is unaddressed.'

## Observed failure mode

Sonnet at iter 2 of axon-43ed769b read the prompt (which had the original description with 'subject' and the unchanged AC), verified all type shapes match, ran tests green, and wrote a no_changes_rationale declaring the work complete. The 'already_satisfied' close path auto-closed the bead. The actual BLOCKING issue (wrong field name) was invisible to the execute-bead layer. I had to manually reopen and rewrite the description with fix-up instructions inline.

## This should be part of the post-review epic scope

Making review findings round-trip through the prompt is half of what 'post-review agent in work' means. Adding to this bead's scope:

- Review verdict JSON includes a 'findings' array with severity (blocking|partial|nit) and location
- Reopen path persists the findings to the bead
- execute-bead prompt rendering pulls latest review findings and injects them as a distinct XML section
- Execute-bead instructions teach the agent to treat blocking findings as first-class work items
- no_changes / already_satisfied paths check for unaddressed blocking findings before closing
</operator-feedback>","owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-451c8bc5","status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in work","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:57:50.41702859Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:57:25.094183105Z"}

3. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, decomposed-child bead IDs, and the execution-ineligible/proposed/dependency-waiting decision.

7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","closing_commit_sha":"c7b3605270fbae8055c09d3e04a656736d208a5f","created_at":"2026-06-08T06:21:15.999176918Z","description":"PROBLEM
A Niflheim decomposed parent bead kept consuming attempts and escalated to stronger routes despite evidence that the bead was already split into executable children. Stronger models cannot implement a parent bead whose correct action is decompose/execution-ineligible/operator work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing or empty decomposition paths can warn and continue.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` recognizes execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says only genuine failure should escalate, but decomposed-parent/no-changes outcomes can still reach implementation retry and min-power escalation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style decomposed-parent fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`, `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`. Classify the parent as execution-ineligible, proposed, dependency-waiting, or operator-attention according to policy before another implementation attempt is scheduled. Preserve child bead evidence in the event/result audit.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`.

A parent bead that has already been decomposed can still re-enter the worker as if it were an executable task. That keeps spending attempts on a structurally ineligible parent instead of moving on to its children.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:130-210` already marks decomposed children with `Parent=beadID` and sets the parent execution-eligible flag to false, but the main worker loop still has paths that continue past decomposition-like intake and retry handling. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles some intake decomposition cases, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3818-3840` continues processing other outcome branches without consistently parking a parent whose own `no_changes` rationale says to decompose.

PROPOSED FIX
- When the worker sees `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`, mark the parent execution-ineligible or proposed before another implementation attempt.
- Do not schedule another implementation run or min-power escalation for that parent.
- Keep the parent visible as a decomposition boundary, not as a retriable implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change child-bead content, land handling, or review gating in this child.
- Do not alter the decomposer output format beyond what is needed to detect the decomposed-parent state.","events_attachment":"ddx-d4817c9b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-d4817c9b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-334c0958","priority":0,"queue-rank":2050,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5e04821f","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: park decomposed parents before retrying implementation","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:35.835830846Z"}

- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1754:{"acceptance":"AC1. Land-coordinator refuses to merge a bead result whose net diff deletes more than N lines from a single file (configurable threshold, default 200) UNLESS the agent's no_changes_rationale.txt or commit message explicitly acknowledges and justifies the deletion (e.g. 'intentional file removal: <reason>').

AC2. Land-coordinator runs a basic sanity gate before merging: the file must still parse for its filetype (TypeScript files compile, Svelte files have valid <script>/<template>/<style> structure if they had one previously, JSON is valid JSON, etc.). Failed parse = preserve as iteration ref, do not merge.

AC3. When a parse-fail or large-deletion gate trips, the bead is marked 'preserved-needs-review' and the operator is alerted via the bead's notes field, with a link to the iteration ref and a one-line summary of what gate fired.

AC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.

AC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.

AC6. A regression test on the harness validates that an attempt to merge a result deleting >N lines (or producing a parse failure) is correctly preserved+rejected, not merged.","archived_at":"2026-07-03T07:18:01Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3881188","closing_commit_sha":"77715556bbfaf9ed63d5b9aa0d23da6ca724b02f","created_at":"2026-04-30T01:17:03.251053789Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-fd31b628","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e2a51cd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e19c5181","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-b6c42808","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:54Z"}],"description":"Today on axon repo, the ddx-land-coordinator merged a bead-result branch onto master that deleted 1448 lines from one Svelte component (1552 → 104 lines). The same merge also corrupted a second test file (1043 → 158 lines, overwritten with unrelated content). Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its <script lang="ts"> wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.

The damaging merge:

  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075
  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2
  Author: ddx-land-coordinator <coordinator@ddx.local>
  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400

      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master

       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------
       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)

The post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.

Net damage on master before manual recovery:
  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)
  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)
  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start

Manual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.

Why this happened (hypothesis):
  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).
  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).
  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.

Or alternatively: the agent's tool calls deleted content from the worktree without re-emitting the full file (e.g. used Edit with overlapping replace blocks that effectively truncated), the harness committed whatever was on disk without sanity-checking magnitude, and the land-coordinator merged the partial content as authoritative.","id":"ddx-efe058be","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:land-coordinator","kind:safety","severity:critical"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: land-coordinator merged a result that deleted 1448 lines from a single file (catastrophic damage)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:22:05.677486788Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44.162888426Z"}

.ddx/executions/20260504T022245-36a2ca7b/prompt.md:164:+    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.\n\nCURRENT STATE (the smell)\n\nEach flag traverses 6 boundaries:\n\n1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX(\"flag\") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)\n2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)\n3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)\n4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)\n5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49\n6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656\n\nALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):\n- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.\n\nTwo of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.\n\nDESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct\n\nDefine one struct, used at every layer:\n\n  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)\n                ↓\n       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)\n                ↓\n       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker\n                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)\n                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)\n                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)\n                                                runWorker(spec)\n\nProperties:\n- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.\n- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).\n- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.\n- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.\n- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.\n\nCRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)\n\n1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.\n\n2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:\n   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied\n   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)\n   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)\n   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed\n   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)\n\n3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion \u003e supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.\n\n4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).\n\nLOCATION\n\nLikely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).\n- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.\n\nUSER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)\n- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.\n- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.\n- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.\n\nABSORBS THE NARROW FIX\nThe 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.\n\nSEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\n\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.\n- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

.ddx/executions/20260504T021759-c712d0e8/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.\n\nCURRENT STATE (the smell)\n\nEach flag traverses 6 boundaries:\n\n1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX(\"flag\") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)\n2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)\n3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)\n4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)\n5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49\n6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656\n\nALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):\n- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.\n\nTwo of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.\n\nDESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct\n\nDefine one struct, used at every layer:\n\n  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)\n                ↓\n       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)\n                ↓\n       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker\n                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)\n                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)\n                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)\n                                                runWorker(spec)\n\nProperties:\n- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.\n- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).\n- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.\n- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.\n- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.\n\nCRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)\n\n1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.\n\n2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:\n   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied\n   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)\n   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)\n   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed\n   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)\n\n3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion \u003e supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.\n\n4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).\n\nLOCATION\n\nLikely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).\n- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.\n\nUSER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)\n- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.\n- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.\n- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.\n\nABSORBS THE NARROW FIX\nThe 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.\n\nSEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\n\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.\n- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓

                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

.ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/prompt.md:5:Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

.ddx/executions/20260503T110552-7129c1c3/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.\n\nCURRENT STATE (the smell)\n\nEach flag traverses 6 boundaries:\n\n1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX(\"flag\") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)\n2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)\n3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)\n4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)\n5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49\n6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656\n\nALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):\n- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.\n\nTwo of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.\n\nDESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct\n\nDefine one struct, used at every layer:\n\n  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)\n                ↓\n       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)\n                ↓\n       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker\n                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)\n                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)\n                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)\n                                                runWorker(spec)\n\nProperties:\n- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.\n- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).\n- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.\n- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.\n- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.\n\nCRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)\n\n1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.\n\n2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:\n   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied\n   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)\n   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)\n   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed\n   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)\n\n3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion \u003e supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.\n\n4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).\n\nLOCATION\n\nLikely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).\n- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.\n\nUSER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)\n- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.\n- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.\n- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.\n\nABSORBS THE NARROW FIX\nThe 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.\n\nSEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\n\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.\n- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.",

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/prompt.md:3:    <title>ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner</title>
.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/prompt.md:5:Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract

- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract

NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)
